source_title: DOW-UAP-D56, Range Fouler Debrief, Arabian Sea, August 2020
description_blurb:
This document is a Range Fouler Debrief Form, a standardized reporting form the U.S. Navy uses to record the circumstances surrounding an unauthorized intrusion into controlled airspace during active military operations or training. These reports contain a narrative description of the observer’s experiences. 
A U.S. military operator reported an encounter with a group of three “unidentified small air contacts” over the North Arabian Sea. The reporter described the UAP as having “wings/airframe” structure, and as initially bearing on a westerly heading. The operator tracked one UAP before losing sight of it behind a cloud. Upon regaining contact, the operator reported observing two additional UAP to the east of the first. The report states that all three objects then “appeared to maintain their relative course, speed, and altitude.”
All descriptive and estimative language contained in this report reflects the reporter’s subjective interpretation at the time of the event. Such characterizations should not be interpreted as a conclusive indication of the presence or absence of any intrinsic object features or performance characteristics.
title: DOW-UAP-D56, Range Fouler Debrief, Arabian Sea, August 2020
summary: A U.S. Navy O-3 Pilot from HSM-73 reported observing three unidentified small air contacts during routine operations in the North Arabian Sea on August 24, 2020. The objects, described as 'round' with 'wings/airframe' structure, were initially seen as one contact before two additional contacts appeared. They were moving on a westerly heading and appeared to maintain their relative course, speed, and altitude. No electronic warfare (ES), radar, or IFF tracks were achieved, and no interaction took place.
reported_object_description: Three unidentified small air contacts, described as 'round' with 'wings/airframe' structure.
reported_behavior: The objects were moving on a westerly heading (270/UNK) and appeared to maintain their relative course, speed, and altitude. One object was initially tracked before being lost behind a cloud, then contact was regained with two additional objects.
bust_reasoning: The bust score of 55 reflects a genuinely uncertain observation. While the single visual sensor and night conditions increase the likelihood of misidentification, the specific description of 'round' with 'wings/airframe' is unusual and not easily reconciled with common mundane objects like simple balloons or lens flares. The lack of radar/IFF is significant but not definitive for anomaly, as small or stealthy objects might evade detection. The behavior described (maintaining relative course, speed, and altitude) is not anomalous, but the sequence of losing one object and then regaining contact with two additional ones adds a layer of ambiguity to the observation's consistency.
full_text:
# DOW-UAP-D56, Range Fouler Debrief, Arabian Sea, August 2020 - Page 1

Declassified by MG Richard A. Harrison
USCENTCOM Chief of Staff
Declassified on: 20 March 2026

Please do not use the purple "submit" button above. Save and email the complete file manually (see below).

[SECRET//NOFORN - Redacted]

# Range Fouler Debrief Form

Please complete this form to the best of your ability. If you do not have the requested information, please leave the field blank. If there was more than one "group," please report each on a separate form for data collection purposes. You should receive a response within 5 business days.

| Last Name, First Name | Rank | Squadron | SIPR Email Address | Crew Position |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| | O-3 | HSM-73 | | Pilot |

This information is for contact only. SPEAR sanitizes all reports of identifying information. Absolutely no identifying information for aircrew or squadron will be recorded for analysis.

| 08/24/20 | 00:04:30 | Night | 705 | 168122 | SSC | No |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Date (mm/dd/yy) | Time of detection (hh:mm:ss Z) | Day / Night | Side No. | Buno | Mission Description (CAS, BFM, etc) | LFE? |

| 21440 | 21440 | [1.4a] | | Yes | 310 | 5 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Contact Working Area (Ex: W-72 1A) | Contact Latitute (DD:MM:SS) | Contact Longitude (DD:MM:SS) | E/W | Contact Altitude (Ex: 22000) | Altitude Constant? | Wind Dir at Contact Alt (From) | Wind Speed |

Please attempt to determine a Lat / Long for the contact at initial detection. We recommend using JMPS to reference the sensor-derived bearing and range relative to the bullseye you had in use. If that method is not available, any reasonable derivation of location will work, but please be as specific as possible and make a note in the comments section. These locations may be used to cue other means of tracking.

**Was the contact moving?** Yes
**Direction/Speed (Ex. 090/15):** 270/UNK

| | | |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Radar Equipped:** Other | **AIM-9x Self-Track:** [ ] | **Please check all that apply:** |
| **Stable Trackfile?:** No | **ATFLIR Autotrack:** [ ] | Round: [x]   Moving Parts: [ ] |
| **# of Contacts in "Group":** 3 | **Tally Achieved:** [ ] | Square: [ ]   Metallic: [ ] |
| **EA Indications (Check all that apply):** | | Balloon-shaped: [ ]   Markings: [ ] |
| [ ] ECM Arc   [ ] Letter Identifier   [ ] False Trackfiles   [ ] Other/Ambiguous | | Wings/Airframe: [x]   Translucent: [ ] |
| | | Other Shape: [ ]   Opaque: [ ] |
| | | Apparent Propulsion: [ ]   Reflective: [ ] |

Please use the field below to describe the contact and any interaction in your own words with as much detail as possible. Please be sure to include any detail not included in questions above.

[1.4a] observed 3x possible unidentified small air contacts while conducting routine operations in the North Arabian Sea. Negative ES, radar track, and IFF track. Distance to contact was unknown. Speed of contact was unknown. Precise course of contact was unknown, but appeared to be on a westerly heading. No interaction took place between [1.4a] and the unknown air contacts. Initially observed 1x unknown air contact and tracked it before losing sight as it went behind a cloud. When contact on the unknown air contact was regained, 2x additional unknown air contacts were seen due east of the location of the initial contact. All 3x unknown air contacts appeared to maintain their relative course, speed, and altitude.

Don't use the purple "submit" button! Save this form with filename "Date_Squadron_RF.pdf" and email it to [redacted] (Also in the global). For troubleshooting, call [redacted].

Thank you for your time. Please ensure all display tapes are ripped for the entire time of interaction and saved as a .wmv (Example: 4 May VFA-106 HUD.wmv). Squadron intel personnel shall upload those files to the repository located at this link.

[redacted]

[SECRET//NOFORN - Redacted]

USCENTCOM MDR 26-0038 to 26-0046          Approved for Release to AARO          03/27/26 000001
cover_up_reasoning: The cover-up score of 55 indicates substantial, though not extreme, concealment. While the document has been declassified, the redaction of specific operational details such as the contact's latitude/longitude (`[1.4a]`) and the internal repository link for uploading display tapes goes beyond routine anonymization of personal identifying information. The original `SECRET//NOFORN` classification also suggests a higher level of sensitivity and restricted access, even if the document is now released.
title_zh: DOW-UAP-D56，靶场干扰物情况汇报，阿拉伯海，2020年8月
summary_zh: 2020年8月24日，美国海军HSM-73中队一名O-3级飞行员在北阿拉伯海例行行动中报告观测到三个不明小型空中目标。这些目标被描述为“圆形”且具有“机翼/机身”结构，最初被视为一个目标，随后又出现两个目标。它们以向西航向移动，并且似乎保持了其相对航向、速度和高度。未能获得电子战（ES）、雷达或敌我识别（IFF）跟踪，且未发生任何互动。
reported_object_description_zh: 三个不明小型空中目标，被描述为“圆形”且具有“机翼/机身”结构。
reported_behavior_zh: 这些目标以向西航向（270/未知）移动，并且似乎保持了其相对航向、速度和高度。最初跟踪到一个目标，随后该目标消失在云层后，之后又重新捕捉到两个目标。
bust_reasoning_zh: 55分的“破解”评分反映了这是一次确实不确定的观测。尽管单一视觉传感器和夜间条件增加了误判的可能性，但“圆形”且具有“机翼/机身”结构的具体描述并不寻常，难以与简单的气球或镜头光晕等常见普通物体相符。缺乏雷达/IFF跟踪具有重要意义，但并非异常现象的决定性证据，因为小型或隐形物体可能规避探测。所描述的行为（保持相对航向、速度和高度）并非异常，但先失去一个目标，随后又重新捕捉到两个目标的序列，为观测的一致性增添了一层模糊性。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 55分的“掩盖”评分表明存在实质性但非极端的隐瞒。尽管该文件已被解密，但对目标经纬度（`[1.4a]`）等具体行动细节以及用于上传显示录像的内部存储库链接的删节，超出了个人身份信息的常规匿名化处理范围。原始的“SECRET//NOFORN”分类也表明了更高的敏感性和受限访问级别，即使该文件现已发布。
claims: Three unidentified small air contacts were observed.
Objects were described as 'round' with 'wings/airframe' structure.
Objects initially appeared on a westerly heading.
Objects maintained their relative course, speed, and altitude.
No electronic warfare (ES), radar, or IFF track was achieved.
The observation was made by a U.S. Navy pilot during routine operations.
claims_zh: 观测到三个不明小型空中目标。
目标被描述为“圆形”且具有“机翼/机身”结构。
目标最初以向西航向出现。
目标保持了其相对航向、速度和高度。
未能获得电子战（ES）、雷达或敌我识别（IFF）跟踪。
该观测由一名美国海军飞行员在例行行动中作出。
bust_explanations: Small drones (military reconnaissance or commercial): The description 'small air contacts' with 'wings/airframe' structure, and the lack of radar/IFF track, are consistent with various types of small drones. The 'round' descriptor could be a visual distortion at night or refer to a specific drone design. The observed behavior of maintaining relative course, speed, and altitude is also plausible for drone operations.

Birds (large flock): At night, a flock of birds could be perceived as 'small air contacts' with 'wings/airframe' structure. The 'round' shape might refer to the overall appearance of a dense flock. The lack of radar/IFF is expected for birds, and their ability to maintain relative course/speed/altitude is typical for flocking behavior. The 'lost and regained with two additional' could be a dynamic observation of a moving flock.

Distant conventional aircraft (misidentified): While 'small' and 'round' are less typical, at night, distant aircraft lights or airframes can be misidentified, especially if observed from an unusual angle. The 'wings/airframe' description fits, but the lack of radar/IFF and the 'round' shape remain problematic unless the aircraft was extremely distant or a specific stealth type.
bust_explanations_zh: 小型无人机（军用侦察或商用）：对“小型空中目标”且具有“机翼/机身”结构的描述，以及缺乏雷达/IFF跟踪，与各种类型的小型无人机相符。“圆形”的描述可能是夜间视觉失真，或指特定的无人机设计。所观测到的保持相对航向、速度和高度的行为也符合无人机操作的合理性。
鸟类（大型鸟群）：在夜间，一群鸟可能被感知为具有“机翼/机身”结构的“小型空中目标”。“圆形”可能指密集鸟群的整体外观。鸟类缺乏雷达/IFF跟踪是预料之中的，它们保持相对航向、速度和高度的能力也是群飞行为的典型特征。“先失去后又捕捉到两个额外目标”可能是对移动鸟群的动态观测。
远距离常规飞机（误判）：尽管“小型”和“圆形”不太典型，但在夜间，远距离飞机的灯光或机身可能被误判，尤其是在异常角度观测时。“机翼/机身”的描述符合，但缺乏雷达/IFF跟踪和“圆形”形状仍然存在问题，除非该飞机距离极远或属于特定隐形类型。
cover_up_indicators: Redaction of internal repository link for uploading display tapes
cover_up_indicators_zh: 对目标活动区域的`[1.4a]`删节
对上传显示录像的内部存储库链接的删节
